negative_cases.why_wrong: Thermometer Bus Encoder: leaves every thermometer output low, so it compiles but fails all nonzero input levels.
Thermometer Bus Encoder: turns on one extra segment for nonzero inputs, passing prefix shape while failing exact segment counts.
Thermometer Bus Encoder: emits binary-style bit values instead of a prefix thermometer word, so sparse smoke can look active but bus semantics fail.
Thermometer Bus Encoder: builds the active prefix from t15 downward, preserving segment count while violating bus order.
Thermometer Bus Encoder: rounds the input bin instead of flooring it, passing endpoints while failing mid-bin hidden samples.
